name: Run a BANT Discovery Call (Budget, Authority, Need, Timing)
description: Run a short qualifying conversation with a live prospect to determine whether their Budget, Authority, Need, and Timing align well enough to justify a follow-up. The goal is honest mutual qualification — not extracting commitment.
disclaimer: Skills assume the call is solicited by the operator. NEVER use these to power a mass-cold-call campaign without the operator's explicit per-prospect authorisation.

context.when_to_use: A prospect has agreed to a discovery / intro call (~15-20 minutes). The operator wants to know whether to invest further sales motion or politely disqualify.
preconditions: Prospect has consented to a discovery call (not ambushed on a cold dial).
Operator has supplied the one-line value prop and the ideal-customer profile.
Agent has researched the prospect's company at the level a peer would expect.
Agent has a graceful out for both 'great fit' and 'not a fit'.

principles: Discovery is mutual. You are qualifying them; they are qualifying you. Both sides should leave with a clearer no or a clearer yes.
Earn each BANT question before asking it. Premature 'what's your budget?' kills the call.
Listen more than you talk. Target 30% agent / 70% prospect by minute count.
Open questions over closed ones. 'What does success look like in 90 days?' opens; 'do you need feature X?' closes.
Disqualifying gracefully is a win. A clean 'we're not the right fit' protects both sides' time and builds long-term trust.
Take notes verbatim where possible — exact quotes from the prospect become the operator's best follow-up material.

phrases.opener: Thanks for the time today. Just to set expectations — I want to use the next 15 or so minutes to understand what you're working on, see if what [operator] does is actually relevant, and if not, save us both the next meeting. Sound good?
phrases.need_open: What's the thing that made you take this call? What's going on right now that made [topic] worth 15 minutes?
phrases.need_deepen: Got it. And what have you tried so far? What's worked, what hasn't?
phrases.authority_soft: If you decided this was worth doing, who else would be involved in the call to bring it forward — anyone we should loop in next?
phrases.timing_open: Where does this sit on your list right now — is it a 'this quarter' thing, a 'thinking about next year' thing, or somewhere in between?
phrases.budget_indirect: Without getting into numbers, is there an existing budget line this would fit into, or would it need to be a new line of spend?
phrases.graceful_disqualify: Based on what you've described, I'm not sure we're the right fit — and I'd rather tell you that now than waste a follow-up. Here's what I think you actually need: [reframe]. Want me to point you in that direction?
phrases.graceful_next_step: This sounds like it's worth a second conversation. Could I propose [specific concrete next step] — would [day/time] work?

tactics.name: Open with a frame, not a pitch
tactics.script: Use the `opener` phrase. Naming 'we might not be a fit and that's fine' lowers the prospect's defences and the conversation gets real fast.

tactics.name: Start with N (Need) — never with B (Budget)
tactics.script: Use `need_open` and follow with `need_deepen`. Spend the first 5-7 minutes here. The other three letters become easy if Need is real and undervague.

tactics.name: Probe Authority gently, never as gatekeeping
tactics.when: Around minute 8-10, after Need is clear.
tactics.script: Use `authority_soft`. Phrase it as 'who else benefits' not 'are you the decision-maker?' — the latter is patronising and produces nothing useful.

tactics.name: Ask Timing before Budget
tactics.script: Use `timing_open`. Timing tells you whether the next step is a follow-up call or a calendared check-in in 2 quarters.

tactics.name: Ask Budget indirectly
tactics.when: Last of the four, only if Need + Timing both pass.
tactics.script: Use `budget_indirect`. You rarely need an exact dollar figure on a discovery call — you need to know if money exists or has to be invented.

tactics.name: Disqualify out loud when it's clear
tactics.when: It's clear they're not a fit AND there's still 5+ minutes left.
tactics.script: Use `graceful_disqualify`. Don't burn the last 5 minutes politely pretending. Disqualify, offer something useful (a referral, a resource), and end early — they will remember it positively.

tactics.name: Close on one concrete next step
tactics.script: Use `graceful_next_step`. One step, with a proposed time. Vague 'I'll send you something' closes drift into ghosting.

boundaries: Do NOT push past 'no budget' by suggesting they reorganise their priorities — that's pressure, not discovery.
Do NOT promise capabilities the operator hasn't authorised you to promise.
Do NOT record or transcribe the call without explicit consent.
Do NOT use BANT as an interrogation — if the prospect resists, switch to open conversation. The framework is internal scaffolding, not a script the prospect has to recognise.
Do NOT continue qualifying after Need has clearly failed. If there's no real problem to solve, you're not their vendor.
success_signals: Prospect describes Need in their own words with specifics (numbers, recent events, named pain).
Prospect names other people on their side ('I'd loop in our CTO and head of ops').
Prospect commits to a concrete next-step time on the call.
Prospect asks pricing-shaped questions of their own initiative.
Prospect references a specific timeline (quarter, fiscal year event).
failure_signals: Need answers stay abstract ('we just want to be more efficient').
Prospect cannot or will not name anyone else who would be involved.
Budget answer is 'we don't really do that kind of spend' AND nothing about Need indicates it's worth changing that.
Prospect is on the call clearly to be polite, not to evaluate.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm the next step verbally, send a calendar invite within 2 hours, and a short recap email within 24 hours with the prospect's own words quoted back.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Politely disqualify on the call. Offer a useful referral if you have one. Send a 2-line follow-up email thanking them and confirming you're not pushing.
follow_ups: Write a one-paragraph CRM note: BANT score per dimension, exact prospect quotes, next step or disqualification reason.
If timing was 'next year', set a calendar reminder to re-contact 30 days before the named window.

required_user_info: Operator's one-line value prop and ideal customer profile
Operator's pricing band (so the agent can interpret budget answers)
What 'qualified' looks like for the operator — minimum thresholds per BANT dimension
Operator's authorised next-step options (demo / proposal / pilot / referral-out)
Whether the agent may quote pricing live or must defer to the operator
